exploration_log.aha_moment: The simdjson_result<array> specialization in array.h also declares all array methods (count_elements, is_empty, etc.) and needs a matching size() declaration and implementation in array-inl.h to be consistent.
exploration_log.hindsight: Should have immediately looked for both the primary class declaration and its simdjson_result specialization to avoid needing two separate rounds of edits.

alternatives: Add size() as a separate implementation body calling count_elements logic directly
key_decisions.decision: Implement size() as a one-liner forwarding to count_elements()
key_decisions.reasoning: count_elements() already has full O(n) loop + rewind logic; size() is purely an alias to avoid code duplication

solve_notes: Added `size()` as a one-line alias for `count_elements()` in both `ondemand::array` (declaration in `array.h`, implementation in `array-inl.h`) and its `simdjson_result<array>` specialization. This provides API symmetry with `dom::array` while reusing all existing O(n) count + rewind logic.
task_text: The `ondemand::array` class does not have a `size()` method, but `dom::array` does. Add a `size()` convenience method to `ondemand::array` that returns the same `simdjson_result<size_t>` as `count_elements()` (i.e., it is an alias with the same O(n) caveat). Update the class declaration in `include/simdjson/generic/ondemand/array.h` and the implementation in `include/simdjson/generic/ondemand/array-inl.h`.
